items.title: Tunisians protest against undocumented sub-Saharan African migrants
items.description: Protesters gathered on Saturday at midday outside the headquarters of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) in the Lac district of Tunis, demanding the deportation of undocumented sub-Saharan African migrants and calling on the organization to leave Tunisia.

items.title: Armenia election: Prime Minister Pashinyan declares victory
items.description: The election result cements Nikol Pashinyan's Westward push away from Russian influence. It is the first vote since a 2023 crushing military defeat by Azerbaijan.

items.title: Peru election result close as vote counting continues
items.description: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

items.title: Nestory Irankunda the face of changing Socceroos at World Cup
items.description: Nestory Irankunda is set for a breakout moment at the FIFA World Cup as the face of a changing Socceroos team featuring six players of African descent.

items.title: Israel, Iran trade fire in first clash since truce
items.description: Iran and Israel on Monday attacked each other's territory for the first time since a shaky ceasefire put five weeks of war on hold, sparking fears the escalation could spark a new full-scale conflict. Iran fired a barrage of missiles at Israel overnight and Israel responded by targeting military sites in the Islamic republic, with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu apparently defying calls by his ally President Donald Trump for restraint.

items.title: West Asia war LIVE: Netanyahu acknowledges halt in fighting with Iran but vows to respond 'with force' to future attacks
items.description: The U.S. President says that the Strait of Hormuz blockade will continue until a 'final deal' is reached

items.title: Private jet crash kills two pilots in Dominican Republic
items.description: Baseball legend Yadier Molina said the plane had been bound for Texas to pick up him and his family and fly them to his native Puerto Rico

items.title: Iran-Israel tensions escalate over Lebanon | Timeline
items.description: Iran has fired missiles at Israel, marking the first such attack in two months since a ceasefire

items.title: How Lebanon became the breaking point for the Iran war ceasefire
items.description: Iran has switched from projecting power via its proxies to using its own firepower to protect them, analysts say.

items.title: Inflation and FIFA price hikes dampen hope of World Cup boost to US economy
items.description: As preparations for the 2026 World Cup heat up, a massive hiring boom is sweeping across the US service industry. But local businesses are growing increasingly worried that skyrocketing costs and FIFA price hikes could ruin what was expected to be a historic economic celebration.

items.title: Five ways Elon Musk's SpaceX upended Wall Street's IPO playbook
Script reveals methods of Cambodian centre scamming Australians
items.description: Australian and Thai police release a script discovered in a Cambodian scam compound, revealing the high-pressure methods used to coerce Australian victims.

items.title: Depression in romantic relationships: You, me and the illness
items.description: Depression can be a huge challenge for a relationship. Loved ones of someone with depression often feel helpless. But there is actually a lot they can do ― and a lot they shouldn't.

items.title: Spain's visitor numbers hit new highs as tourists avoid Middle East
items.description: The European country had 9.1 million international visitors in April, the most ever for that month.

items.title: Armenia, once Russia's reliable ally, considers an EU future
items.description: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

items.title: Uber opens sign-ups for London 'robotaxis' ahead of launch 'in months'
Three more teens charged over alleged stabbing of homeless man
items.description: Police charge a further three teens following the alleged stabbing of a homeless man in the regional Victorian city of Bendigo last week.

items.title: Armenian PM Pashinyan declares victory in parliamentary elections
items.description: Armenian PM Pashinyan declares victory in parliamentary elections

items.title: Can Xi bring North Korea closer into Beijing's orbit?
items.description: The Chinese leader's first trip to North Korea in 7 years comes as Pyongyang has moved closer to Russia. Kim Jong Un is likely to seek more access to China's economic power, analysts say.

items.title: Police offer $20,000 reward for information leading to arrest in the killing of Indian national in U.S.
items.description: The Consulate General of India in New York expressed condolences at the tragic incident and said it is extending all possible assistance to Kuncha’s family

items.title: ‘Lives turned in a second’: Family of baby Sam, shot dead by Israel, grieve
items.description: A father speaks of the moment his seven-month-old Palestinian son was shot dead in the West Bank by an Israeli soldier.

items.title: Zelensky's close European allies set out five conditions for peace talks
items.description: The Ukrainian president's meeting with his staunchest European allies came as US President Donald Trump's focus shifts to the war with Iran.

items.title: India, China should stick to ‘right strategic perception’ of being cooperative partners: Beijing
items.description: The two sides need to handle the bilateral relations from a strategic height and long-term perspective, expand cooperation, and promote steady development of China-India relations, says top Chinese official

items.title: Squid fleets exposed: Investigation reveals illegal fishing and forced labour
items.description: June 8 marks World Oceans Day, a global event held every year to raise awareness of the impact of human actions on the ocean. Illegal fishing is one of them, threatening entire marine ecosystems. An investigation published by the non-profit Environmental Justice Foundation reveals widespread environmental destruction and human rights abuses across the global squid industry. Our Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe tells us more.

items.title: IAEA chief says Iran-US nuclear talks in ‘complicated phase’
items.description: IAEA chief says Iran-US nuclear talks in 'complicated phase'.

items.title: Philippines lifts tsunami alert after deadly 7.8 earthquake
items.description: The Philippines and Indonesia both issued tsunami warnings after a powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck off Mindanao. At least 32 people were killed and more than 200 injured.

items.title: At least 11 killed in Pakistan-administered Kashmir clashes before rally
items.description: Police crack down down on supporters of the Joint Awami Action Committee, which plans to hold a rally Tuesday.

items.title: Knicks' long-awaited NBA run electrifies NYC, with Trump set to attend key game
items.description: The Knicks' stunning winning streak has energised the city as the New York basketball team prepares for the third game of the finals series at its home arena.

Spacecraft developer Quantum Space to go public in $1.2 billion SPAC deal
Lebanese 'stuck between a rock and a hard place' as Israel strikes south of country
items.description: Despite Lebanese authorities insisting on the ceasefire, many in Lebanon criticise them for bending the knee to the US. The Hezbollah is also criticised for starting the conflict and siding with Iran, a decision that has impacted almost a third of the country. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis tells us more on the situation in Lebanon.

items.title: What is wrong with the 2026 World Cup
items.description: The 2026 World Cup has come under fire even before kickoff. Criticism is mounting over US President Donald Trump’s politics, high ticket prices, the expansion to 48 teams as well as its environmental impact.

items.title: French star Patrick Bruel placed in police custody over rape, sex assault allegations
items.description: Famed French singer and actor Patrick Bruel has been ​placed in police custody over allegations of rape, attempted rape, ​sexual assault and harassment from 13 victims, ‌the Nanterre prosecution office ⁠said on Monday. Bruel has publicly denied wrongdoing.

items.title: Town saved from flooding 70 years ago sets vintage tractor record
items.description: A New South Wales town hosts a celebration of the "little grey" tractors that helped save it from flooding 70 years ago.

items.title: 'A World Cup for them not us': Fans' anger at US travel bans and visa restrictions
items.description: Fans across the world say US travel bans and visa regulations make them feel excluded from the World Cup.

items.title: Toronto prepares to welcome 300,000 World Cup visitors
items.description: Canada’s largest city will host six World Cup games, starting Friday when the host country’s team takes on Bosnia and Herzegovina

items.title: Iran’s top negotiator threatens U.S. targets over Lebanon escalation
items.description: The comments from ​Iran’s ‌Parliament speaker, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, follow Israeli attacks on the southern ‌suburbs of Lebanon’s capital, ​a stronghold of Iran’s ally ⁠Hezbollah.

items.title: French NATO jet shoots down drone over Latvia as another explodes in Moldova
items.description: A drone that entered NATO member Latvia's airspace from Russia on Monday was shot down by a French military fighter jet, as fragments of another drone were found in a field in Moldova after it entered from Ukraine earlier the same day. Military drones straying into the airspace of ​Russia's neighbours have stoked concerns that the war in Ukraine is spilling over NATO's borders.

items.title: U.K. PM Keir Starmer set to ban ‘harmful’ social media for under-16s
items.description: Keir Starmer is said to have decided to proceed with restrictions after speaking to bereaved ‌parents and considering evidence from Australia, which brought in a ban for under-16s last ‌December

items.title: Hong Kong proposes to let leader John Lee decide what counts as national security offence
items.description: After massive democracy protests rocked the Asian financial hub in 2019, Beijing imposed a national security law that has been used to arrest many leading activists

items.title: As climate shifts, malaria gains ground in southern Africa
items.description: According health officials, climate change is supercharging existing hotspots and lengthening transmission windows, fuelling far more intense outbreaks

items.title: Writer and journalist Mursal Sayas on Afghanistan's child marriage epidemic
items.description: There are real fears that up to 70 percent of girls will soon be in early or forced marriages in Afghanistan. To make matters worse, a new law now means that divorce at any future stage would be totally impossible if the husband were to disagree. Activists say this shameful new law will further place girls and women in a subordinate, legally unequal position to men. Mursal Sayas, a writer, journalist and founder of the NGO Women Beyond Borders told us more in Perspective.

items.title: Iran releases footage of missiles launched at Israel
items.description: Iran said its attacks were "the beginning of a full week of continuous strikes."

items.title: Trump is remaking U.S. policy in Latin America
items.description: Military strikes, sanctions and political pressure are becoming hallmarks of Trump's Latin America policy. NPR's Adrian Florido speaks with historian Greg Grandin about what's driving the shift.

items.title: Israel kills 14 people in attacks across Gaza
items.description: Israel killed at least 14 people in Gaza on Sunday in strikes across Khan Younis, Gaza City, and Deir el-Balah.

items.title: Despite tension over war flare-up, fallout between Trump and Netanyahu unlikely
items.description: Israel and Iran exchanged fire for the first time since the start of the truce on June 7, despite US President Donald Trump repeatedly warning Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu against launching strikes on Iran. Could that be the first sign of a fallout between the two leaders? FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more.

items.title: Friendship or leverage: Why is Xi Jinping in North Korea?
items.description: Beijing is trying to reassert influence over a strategically vital yet deeply unpredictable partner.

items.title: Sri Lankan opposition leaders launch protest in support of former intelligence chief
items.description: Suresh Sallay was admitted to the National Hospital last night due to his resorting to a fast unto death, alleging degrading treatment under detention since late February

items.title: Iran and Israel bomb each other: Is the ceasefire over?
items.description: Analysts say both sides are pushing the ceasefire to its limits while seeking greater leverage.

items.title: Which European nation has the best chance to win World Cup 2026?
items.description: Europe has the most World Cup winners in the event's history, but what are the continent's chances at the 2026 edition?

items.title: Lebanon Latest: Israel pushes deeper as conflict escalates
items.description: The latest developments from southern Lebanon amid escalating Israeli attacks.

items.title: Nigerian army frees 360 people abducted by Boko Haram
items.description: The Nigerian army said Sunday it freed 360 people abducted by Boko Haram in southern Borno, in the northeastern part of the country.

items.title: 'Hidden cameras' captured Perth paediatrician's alleged sexual offending, court hears
items.description: A paediatrician charged with child sex offences may have up to nine victims, with alleged offending captured on "hidden cameras" in consulting rooms, a Perth court hears.

items.description: Sonko alluded to his split with Faye in a speech broadcast by the Pastef party.

items.title: Eni and Petronas launch gas joint venture in Southeast Asia
Latvia: NATO jet shoots down stray drone
items.description: For the first time, a NATO jet shot down a drone over eastern Latvia. According to the military, the drone entered Latvian airspace due to "Russian electromagnetic warfare."

items.title: Sentencing of officer Benedict Bryant a rare moment of accountability
items.description: Inside a quiet district courtroom in the heart of Sydney’s bustling CBD, a historic moment has unfolded involving a NSW Police sergeant.

items.title: Activists protests over Nairobi National Park plan
items.description: Environmental activists and conservationists staged a protest in Nairobi on Monday against plans to construct a parking facility inside Nairobi National Park.

items.title: Pope urges Spanish Church to adopt ‘culture of care’, reparations for sex abuse survivors
items.description: Pope Leo XIV told Spanish bishops on Monday to provide reparations and adopt a "culture of care" ahead of an expected meeting with survivors of clergy sexual abuse during his weeklong visit to the country. The Spanish hierarchy had largely dismissed the scale of abuse in their church for decades until a newspaper began documenting a legacy of abuse and cover-up.

items.title: Local mayor 'shocked' at man's death after 'ineffective' taser arrest
items.description: A local mayor has expressed shock at the death of a man during an arrest at Clare, in South Australia's Mid North on Sunday, as the state's police minister says he is confident the incident will be thoroughly investigated.

items.title: United Nations urge Afghan Taliban to end arrests of women over dress rules
items.description: The United Nations ⁠Assistance Mission in Afghanistan did not specify how many women had been affected

items.title: West Asia war updates: Iran launches missiles at Israel
items.description: Israel military chief vows to strike Iran 'with force as soon as greenlight is given'; Iran's Revolutionary Guards said that the missile attack on Israel was a "warning" after the Israeli strike on Beirut on Sunday

items.title: Donald Trump dismisses idea that Iran betrays his ‘no new wars’ campaign message
items.description: ‘I don't like these endless wars; this is not an endless war; we've been doing this for three months,” says U.S. President Donald Trump

items.title: Iran says it has halted attacks on Israel after first exchange of fire since truce
items.description: Iran launched about 30 missiles at Israel following a strike in Lebanon, while Israel carried out two waves of air strikes in Iran.

items.title: Marvell shares jump after chipmaker wins spot in S&P 500
South Africa's President Ramaphosa moves to diffuse anti-immigrant tensions in TV address
items.description: South Africa's president promised Sunday to act on what he called concerns over illegal migration following a rise in anti-immigrant protests and sentiment in Africa's most advanced economy, with other nations claiming their citizens have been targeted in xenophobic attacks.

items.title: French justice system 'overwhelmed': 'Systemic' failures tackling violence against women & children
items.description: Annette Young is pleased to welcome Laura Slimani, Director of Projects at the Women's Foundation, a French NGO which has been very much focused on ending violence against women and girls. The killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna has ignited a wave of public anger across France, prompting demonstrations in more than 200 cities and renewed scrutiny of how the justice system handles violence against women and children. Ms. Slimani argues that the tragedy is not simply the result of an individual failure but rather it "reveals a much broader dysfunction and systemic crisis in the entire French justice system: judicial delays, chronic underfunding, and a widening gap between reports of sexual violence and successful prosecutions.

items.title: Ebola outbreak spreading at an unprecedented pace, Africa CDC says
items.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo's Ebola outbreak is spreading at an unprecedented pace, Africa CDC warns.

items.title: Lentils are healthy, cheap and versatile, and they shine in these recipes
items.description: From soups to bolognese and oven bakes, lentils are a versatile and affordable meal option.

items.title: UK, Germany, France back ceasefire talks between Ukraine and Russia
items.description: The British, German and French leaders backed a call by Ukraine's Zelenskyy for direct talks with Russian President Putin. The European leaders met Zelenskyy in London.

items.title: Kosovo PM wins snap vote, but with no majority
items.description: Sunday's vote, the country's third in just over a year, was marked by low turnout. The country's prolonged political crisis has further debilitated an already ailing economy.

items.title: Celebrities slip and slide in first Big Freeze event since Neale Daniher's death
items.description: The MCG has hosted the first Big Freeze MND fundraiser since the death of AFL legend and motor neurone disease campaigner, Neale Daniher.

items.title: Fast Money Music on his debut album and love for France
items.description: US citizen Nick Hinman moved to London six years ago, where he nurtured his music project Fast Money Music – a name partly inspired by New York band Suicide's track of the same name. He popped by the arts24 studio to tell Marjorie Hache about his influences and working with band members from Klaxons and The 1975. They also take a look at new releases by Olivia Rodrigo, Fatoumata Diawara and Vince Staples.

items.title: Calls for kindness as community mourns death of shark attack victim
items.description: The Albany community is still coming to terms with the loss of resident Daniel Turpin after he did not survive a shark attack at the weekend.

items.title: Dissident Chinese artist revisits Monet's waterlilies through Lego art
items.description: At the MuMa du Havre, Chinese artist Ai Weiwei revisits Monet's famous Water lilies through two monumental works composed of 650,000 Lego bricks. Behind the technical prowess is also a more intimate story, marked by exile, dissent - and the artist's family history.

items.title: Why was Israel spared scrutiny for the 1967 USS Liberty attack?
items.description: US Representative Massie set to honour the crew of the vessel on the House floor, bringing attention to the 1967 attack.

items.title: Cerebras shares climb as Wall Street brokerages back AI chip strategy
A 'ring of fire' could soon burn around one of Australia's major cities
items.description: Australian states are pushing ahead with waste-to-energy incineration despite significant opposition. It means one capital city will soon be surrounded by a "ring of fire".

items.title: Rare footage captured of Great White shark in Mediterranean Sea
items.description: A volunteer diver has described shaking as he filmed his encounter with an endangered Great White shark between Tunisia and Sicily.

Rider killed in Finke Desert Race crash identified as Matthew Bielefeld
items.description: A motorcyclist who died while competing in this weekend's Finke Desert Race outside Alice Springs has been identified as Matthew Bielefeld.

items.title: DR Congo court sentences 54 to death in final verdict on murder of UN experts
items.description: Nearly nine years after the killing of two United Nations experts in the Democratic Republic of Congo, the country's High Military Court has delivered its final verdict.

items.title: Taiwan says China Coast Guard patrols to its east are 'provocative act'
items.description: China, which views democratically governed Taiwan as its own territory, was angered after Japan and the Philippines said last month they would ‌begin formal talks on delimiting their maritime boundaries, viewing that as involving waters off Taiwan

items.title: French government 'under pressure' amid public outcry over children protection failures
items.description: The French Justice minister convened public prosecutors on June 8. Gerald Darmanin told them they would need to review some 70 thousand cases concerning children by July 14. It comes after the body of an 11-year-old girl was found several days after she went missing at the end of May. The main suspect had been accused of sexually abusing children, prompting many to question the failures in France's judicial system. FRANCE 24's Marc Perelman tells us how that could count towards next year's presidential election.

items.title: French Open: Germany's Zverev wins first ever Grand Slam after defeating Italy's Cobolli
items.description: Alexander Zverev has won his first Grand Slam title at the fourth attempt, beating Flavio Cobolli in a five-set epic in Paris. He is the first German man to win a major singles title in 30 years.

items.title: Mexico City attempts record-breaking wave
items.description: As part of the countdown to the World Cup, Mexico City is attempting to surpass the world record for largest wave.

items.title: Hundreds of captives freed from Boko Haram mountain hideout
items.description: The group includes many women and children who were abducted in March from an area close to Cameroon.

items.title: Oil prices rise more than $2 on Israel strikes on Lebanon
items.description: U.S. crude ‌futures were up $2.10, or 2.32%, at $92.64 per barrel as of 0013 ‌GMT, ⁠while Brent crude futures rose $2.33, or 2.5%, to $95.42 ⁠a barrel

items.title: Pakistani workers say their faith cost them their jobs in the UAE
items.description: Shia Muslims from Pakistan say they're being deported from the UAE shortly after the Iran war began.

items.title: Ousted Senegal PM Ousmane Sonko re-elected party chief amid crisis
items.description: The speaker of Senegal's parliament, Ousmane Sonko, a powerful mentor-turned-rival to President Bassirou Diomaye Faye, won re-election Saturday as head of their party amid a political crisis sparked by their falling-out.

items.description: “Of the 19 confirmed cases so far, 14 were among people who entered from DRC and five are Ugandan nationals,” Mr. Tedros said on X

items.title: Peru: Presidential election too close to call
items.description: With over 90% of the votes counted in Peru's runoff presidential election, right-wing conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori and left-wing politician Roberto Sanchez are in a neck-and-neck race.

items.title: Iraqi officials work to identify victims after bus crash kills 21
items.description: A passenger bus crashed and burst into flames June 7 in southern Iraq, in the latest deadly accident on the country's poorly maintained roads

items.title: 'Once Trump accepted Iranian 14-point proposal as basis of talks, he gave Iran political initiative'
items.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Scott Lucas, Professor of American Studies and International Politics at the Clinton Institute, University College Dublin. Amid the latest escalation between Iran, Israel, and the US, Lucas warns that both Tehran and Jerusalem are increasingly willing to act independently of Washington's preferences, exposing what he describes as Donald Trump's diminishing ability to dictate events. He also argues that Tehran is actively seeking to reshape the diplomatic framework, insisting on linking developments in Lebanon, the Strait of Hormuz, sanctions relief, and regional security into a single bargaining process.

items.title: Texas-bound jet crashes during emergency landing in Dominican Republic
items.description: Texas-bound private jet crashes during emergency landing in Dominican Republic.

items.description: Markets in Asia are hit by a tech sell-off, and oil is volatile as Iran and Israel launch attacks on each other.

items.description: In a Tehran neighbourhood dotted with shops selling football jerseys and sporting goods, 10-year-old Helma was already feeling World Cup fever.

items.description: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

items.description: The attack comes after Hezbollah struck Israel's military with fighter drones, according to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office.

Despite this, strikes on Israel from Hezbollah are still ongoing, as FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnapolsky reports from Jerusalem.

items.description: Nearly three years after a catastrophic storm tore through eastern Libya's port city of Derna killing almost 4,000 people the city is getting back on its feet, but the trauma persists.

items.description: The magnitude-7.8 quake triggered small tsunami waves in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan.

items.description: Video shows plumes of smoke rising from Mahshahr Petrochemical Complex in Iran’s Khuzestan Province on Monday.

items.title: 'Pizza connection' between German conservatives and Greens
items.description: Thirty years ago, rivaling politicians from Germany's conservative CDU and the Greens met to discuss their differences ― and what they had in common. The pizza connection was born. Now that format is being revived.

items.description: Sammy Wurrawilya has taken out the title of spear-throwing champion at this year's Barunga Festival with an impressive effort of more than 30 metres.

items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.description: In view of the latest developments in the region, the Embassy advises all Indian nationals to avoid travel to Iran and those currently in Iran to exit the country using available transport

items.title: Can Hawaii wean itself off oil imports?
items.description: Hawaii imports much of its fuel — and pays the price. From solar to geothermal, the state is searching for a way out of fossil fuel dependence.

items.title: SSRI antidepressants are often 'carelessly prescribed'
items.description: SSRIs, like Prozac and Zoloft, are among the world's most-prescribed psychiatric drugs. These antidepressants help a lot of people. But they don't always work. In fact, some experts suggest they are overprescribed.

items.title: Successive elections take a hit on Kosovo's coffers
items.description: Sunday's election in Kosovo — the third in under 16 months — is marked by a clash between former allies PM Albin Kurti and ex-President Vjosa Osmani, deadlock in the dialogue with Serbia, and obstacles to EU integration.

items.title: Al-Qaeda-linked armed group JNIM releases propaganda video of Mali attack
items.description: The Al-Qaeda-linked armed group Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) has released a propaganda video of the attack on 25 April 2026 against the Malian army camp and the airport in Sevaré, near Mopti, in central Mali.

items.title: First warnings and the myth of ignorance

items.title: Middle East updates: Iran declares end to attacks on Israel
items.description: Iran's military said it would halt its operations against Israel, but warned that strikes would resume if Israel attacked Lebanon. The announcement came after Trump called for an end to the fighting. Follow DW for more.

items.title: Pointe-Noire celebrates African textiles at 13th International Fashion Carousel
items.description: Pointe-Noire, the economic capital of the Republic of Congo, welcomed designers, models and fashion enthusiasts from across the continent for the 13th edition of the Carrousel International de la Mode, held from June 4 to 7, 2026.

items.title: Germany's UN defeat: What went wrong?
items.description: Austria and Portugal have won seats on the UN Security Council, ending Germany's 40-year streak. DW examines the foreign policy questions raised by Berlin's defeat.

items.title: Watch: Donald Trump storms out of NBC interview
items.description: Donald Trump walked out of an interview with NBC’s Meet the Press after he repeatedly made claims that the US 2020 presidential elections was rigged. The US president’s abrupt exit came during a tense exchange between himself and NBC’s Kristen Welker during the interview in Wisconsin.

items.title: News24 | Ceasefire in name only: Why powerful nations keep bombing and face no consequences
items.description: On Wednesday, Israel and Lebanon announced yet another ceasefire – after they had seemingly already agreed to a truce on 16 April.

items.title: Report: CAR to accept third-country deportees from US
items.description: Central African Republic is the latest African nation to agree to accept third-country deportees from the United States, according to a report from Reuters.

items.title: U.K., France, Germany back direct Ukraine-Russia talks
items.description: The leaders “supported the proposal for a direct dialogue between Ukraine and Russia - with active U.S. and European participation - to bring about a ceasefire and support further negotiations,” they said in a joint statement with Mr. Zelenskyy.

items.title: Trump abruptly ends NBC interview after clash over 'rigged election' claim
items.description: During the interview, the president was repeatedly challenged on several points by the show's presenter Kristen Welker.

items.title: 7.8 magnitude quake in Philippines kills at least 35, collapses buildings, sparks tsunami
items.description: Several mostly low-rise buildings collapsed or sustained heavy damage in the hard-hit city of General Santos. Tsunami damage was reported in at least one southern coastal village. Smaller waves were measured in Indonesia and Palau, and as far away as southern Japan

items.title: Crude oil jumps over 3% as Middle East tensions escalate

items.title: DRC: Local authorities, church impose certain Ebola restriction measures
items.description: Solange Hahati, a bride, said that they had invited 300 people to her wedding, but when they arrived at the church, the church authorities allowed only 50 people to enter.

items.title: Iran's World Cup team arrives in Mexico amid US visa row
items.description: Tension between the United States and Iran is continuing in sport. Iran's World Cup squad landed in Mexico on June 7 under the shadow of a bitter diplomatic dispute. With some members of the entourage still lacking U.S. visas. The dispute comes just days before the kickoff of the 2026 World Cup on June 11, which is being jointly hosted by the United States, Mexico and Canada.

items.title: Israel and Iran exchange missile fire threatening Middle East truce

The summit comes at a time when China's economy, strengthened by growing trade and military ties to Russia, could boost ⁠North Korea's confidence in talks.

items.title: Reports: Iran uses war as cover for increased executions

items.title: It’s a small World (Cup)

items.title: 'Democratic backsliding': Armenia's 'extreme polarisation, hate speech, stigmatization' mar election
items.description: FRANCE 24's Charles Pellegrin is pleased to welcome Dr Narek Sukiasyan, Senior Policy Researcher at Friedrich Ebert Stiftung Armenia and Adjunct Lecturer at American University of Armenia. He argues that while Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's victory does signal a continued ouverture towards the West 'in the shape and form of the European Union" he must carefully manage Armenia's dependence on Russia. The election was about far more than foreign policy, explains Dr. Sukiasyan. Security, social concerns, economic vulnerability, and the legacy of conflict were equally central to voters' decisions. Armenia navigating multiple transitions at once: between war and peace, dependence and diversification, polarization and democratic consolidation.

items.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's party won 49.8% of votes, enough to secure a parliamentary majority under Armenia's electoral system.

items.title: Hunger in Oleshky: Ukraine asks for evacuation
items.description: The Ukrainian city of Oleshky has been occupied by Russian troops since 2022. Many residents are cut off from the outside world and living in dire conditions. Now, Kyiv wants to rescue them.

items.title: Algeria, Austria and Jordan – a look at Argentina’s Group J opponents
items.description: Albiceleste will face Algeria, Austria and Jordan at the group stage of the 2026 World Cup; Defending champions are the clear favourites to progress in top spot. Leer más

items.title: Netanyahu acknowledges halt in fighting with Iran but vows to respond 'with force' to future attacks
items.description: In a videotaped statement, Mr. Netanyahu said the fighting stopped after Israel "hit the terror regime in Tehran.” But he said if Iran “makes the mistake and returns to attacking us, we will respond with force,”

items.title: In speech to Spanish parliament, pope demands respect for the dignity of all people
items.description: In the first papal address to the Spanish legislature, the American pope said a "moral renewal" was necessary in legislatures and public life to ensure respect for the inherent dignity of all people.

items.title: Anti-migrant violence leaves even South Africans living in fear
items.description: The coastal town of Mossel Bay along South Africa’s Western Cape was the site of last week’s anti-migrant rampage. Nineteen-year-old South African Nhlamulo Sambo died amid that violence. His family say he was attacked because he was Tsonga and blame his death on what they call a “tribal war."

items.title: Pope Leo XIV praises Spain for its stance on Gaza, Iran, and Ukraine
items.description: Pope Leo XIV has praised Spain for its stance on Gaza, Iran, and Ukraine, and for welcoming migrants, at the start of a weeklong state visit that marks the first papal trip to the country in fifteen years.

items.title: Milei suffers split in his party over judge nomination row
items.description: La Libertad Avanza caucus and opposition senators reached consensus to clear vote on 74 nominations; Controversial attempt to revert on María Verónica Michelli, vetoed by the Executive Branch after initial approval, comes after week of ruling party turmoil. Leer más

items.title: Yemen's Houthis declare 'total ban' on Israeli ships in Red Sea
items.description: The Houthis, who joined the West Asia war in support of Iran in March, had not announced a missile attack on Israel since a fragile ceasefire began on April 8

items.title: Pancreatic cancer: New drug extends lives but isn't a cure
items.description: The newly developed treatment daraxonrasib can double the years left to live for patients with pancreatic cancer — but it doesn't cure the disease and only works if the cancer is treated early.

items.title: IMF report notes concern over corruption in Argentina, delay in asset declarations

items.title: Argentina’s dollar buying tops US$10 billion goal on export boom

items.title: Displaced migrants leave South Africa after xenophobic attacks
items.description: Displaced migrants following xenophobic violence in South Africa’s Western Cape province left the region by bus on Thursday.

items.title: Xi and Kim express hopes for greater ties between China and North Korea
items.description: Xi traveled to Pyongyang on Monday in a likely attempt to reassert China's unique influence over its socialist neighbor.

items.title: Australia, Canada, Japan trade bodies fret over being left out in made-in-Europe drive

items.description: A government tender in Argentina underscored tensions between Washington and Beijing in Latin America as President Javier Milei’s administration awarded a 25-year contract to upgrade a key trade artery to a venture with a history of ties to China. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Mali junta offers $3.5m reward for Sahel al-Qaeda leader
items.description: Mali’s military government is offering $3.5 million for information leading to the arrest or killing of Iyad Ag Ghaly. The junta is also offering smaller rewards for other suspects, including Tuareg separatists.

items.title: Talks begin in Cairo on advancing fragile Gaza ceasefire
items.description: Despite the Gaza truce technically in effect since October, daily violence has rocked the territory, over half of which is under Israeli military control in defiance of the ceasefire's terms

items.title: Court clears implementation of Milei’s Glacier Law reform in Santa Cruz Province
items.description: Appeals court overturns injunction in Santa Cruz Province, allowing Milei-backed mining reform to take effect while broader legal challenges continue. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Earthquake kills 15 in Philippines, people urged to evacuate coastal areas
items.description: A 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck the southern Philippines, killing at least 15 people, collapsing buildings and sparking tsunami warnings across the region.

items.title: News24 | Botswana says US-disabled oil tanker was flying false flag
items.description: Botswana says an oil tanker disabled by the US in the Persian Gulf was falsely registered under its flag, highlighting a common tactic that often uses African nations to obscure the identity of ships.

items.title: Town marches in honour of girl whose killing sparked public outcry
items.description: In the southwestern French town of Fleurance , the parents of 11-year-old Lyhanna joined thousands of people on June 7 for a silent march in her memory. There is growing outrage at systemic failures in the judicial system blamed for the girl's suspected murder. Her body was found last week after she went missing on May 29 near Fleurance. The main suspect had previously been accused of sexually abusing children.

items.title: German asylum benefits cuts violate EU law, top court rules
items.description: In a victory for rejected asylum-seekers, the European Court of Justice has ruled they must be given more than just the bare minimum to survive. But as the EU's new migration pact kicks in, will relief be short-lived?

items.title: Anger as it emerges suspect in teenager's killing attempted previous kidnap
items.description: Details of shocking murder of 14-year-old Agostina Vega published by local media; Wave of public indignation over femicide and authorities’ previous handling of previous case involving prime suspect. Leer más

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Byron Bay park bashing leaves Canadian tourist with brain bleed
items.description: A 28-year-old Canadian tourist is in hospital with fractured skull after a brutal assault by strangers in a park at Byron Bay.

items.title: US$2.5 million, drugs found in raid on top Argentine official’s home
items.description: Facundo Leal, former head of the ARSAT state satellite firm and ORSNA airport regulator, arrested after a raid on his Palermo flat; Over US$2.5 million in cash, drugs and other items, including surveillance gear, seized as part an ongoing court investigation. Leer más

items.title: Milei promises tech firms new laws and ‘unregulated’ AI in Argentina
items.description: President makes bold pitch in 'Financial Times' op-ed, promising to keep AI free from regulation in Argentina; Op-ed is latest in series of moves promoting Argentina as nation friendly to Artificial Intelligence. Leer más

items.description: Government awards concession for dredging and buoy maintenance on the Hidrovía Paraná-Paraguay waterway to consortium comprising Belgian company Jan De Nul and local firm Servimagnus. Leer más

items.title: Amazon, Corning sign multi-billion-dollar deal to boost fiber optics manufacturing in US

items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: EU seeking ways to speed up Western Balkans membership
items.description: European Council President Antonio Costa said new ways need to be found to speed up Western Balkans membership in the EU. His comments come ahead of an upcoming summit in Montenegro.

items.title: Jafar Panahi's prison sentence confirmed by Iranian court
items.description: Following a retrial for a verdict that had been reached while the prominent Iranian director was abroad, the Revolutionary Court in Tehran has upheld a one-year jail sentence.

items.title: News24 | Claws for concern: Bear sightings shut nearly 100 schools as hunters comb Japan streets
items.description: A bear roaming the streets of a Japanese city for three days forced the closure of nearly 100 schools, as dozens of hunters and officials searched for the animal.

items.title: Kings and Queens of the Desert crowned as outback Finke race wraps up
items.description: Returning legends of the Finke Desert Race, David Walsh and Travis Robinson, have been crowned Kings of the Desert at the gruelling two-day off-road race, with Walsh winning on bikes for a record-equalling sixth time and Robinson claiming victory on cars.

items.title: News24 | PHOTOS | UK royals celebrate as Princess Anne’s son weds
items.description: King Charles III and senior British royals gathered on Saturday to celebrate the wedding of Princess Anne’s businessman son, Peter Phillips, to nurse Harriet Sperling.

items.title: Eriksen conscious after collapsing in Denmark game
items.description: Christian Eriksen collapsed during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine but is now conscious, says the Danish Football Association.

items.title: Germany: Bavarian lake procession marks centuries-old boat tradition for Corpus Christi
items.description: Hundreds of worshippers gathered in Bavaria on 4 June for Seehausen's traditional Corpus Christi lake procession. The event, held annually since 1935, combines religious devotion with local heritage on the waters of Staffelsee.

items.title: Germans at Russia's SPIEF: Who's really there?
items.description: Russian officials and media claim German businesses are comming back to the St. Petersburg Economic Forum despite the war in Ukraine. But who is actually attending Russia's flagship event this year?

items.title: Apple WWDC 2026 LIVE | What to expect and where to watch the keynote
items.description: Apple's WWDC this year runs between June 8 and June 12, and starts today at 22:30 IST. The key question for WWDC 2026 is whether or not the AI-enhanced Siri is finally ready to take the stage.

items.title: News24 | Norway’s crown princess faces lung transplant wait as royal family rallies by her side
items.description: Norway’s Crown Princess Mette-Marit, who suffers from an incurable lung disease, has been placed on a waiting list for a lung transplant.

items.title: Clinic in DR Congo's Ebola epicenter 'overwhelmed' with patients
items.description: A hospital in the Ebola-hit region of Bunia in the Democratic Republic of Congo is overwhelmed by the huge number of patients.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.
